phases.name: Opening
markers: audibility check ('can you hear me?' / 'am I audible?') — opens the majority of calls, often repeated through network trouble
camera-on / video-on request before anything else
counsellor self-introduction with title and social proof ('senior academic counsellor', '6,000+ students counselled')
greeting student by name and confirming email ID / application for the Business Analytics & AI programme
congratulating the student on being shortlisted
agenda-setting: listing topics to be covered (program, curriculum, fee structure, faculty, timing)
audio/network troubleshooting, reconnects, 'offers to call back'

phases.name: Discovery
markers: request for self-introduction and background ('asks for student self-introduction', graduation, current work)
reason-for-interest probing ('asks reason for interest', 'why you are applying')
checking parental awareness early ('whether parents know about the course' and fees)
current activity / job / salary questions ('I get here around 90 to 1 lakh... after rent and living cost')
career-goal framing from the student ('I would like to bridge a gap between AI and my work')
surfacing prior research ('any queries from your end which you want to ask and clarify')

phases.name: Presentation
markers: structured module/curriculum walkthrough, screen share or PPT ('once you confirm the seat... you will be able to download this PPT')
faculty and brand credibility ('The faculties are by the Bits only... admission process which is done by the Maasai school')
placement statistics and packages ('the average package is like 11 to 13', 'college dropout... was able to get 8 LPA')
worked analogies explaining the analyst role ('January you had a revenue of 40 lakh... as a business analyst you will be analyzing the business')
certification-value framing ('if you don't have any certification... strong profile')
selectivity proof ('41,000 students applied... only 3,800 students got shortlisted, and we only have 200 seats')

phases.name: Objections & Negotiation
markers: affordability pushback ('the fee is too, like, too much for me to afford')
parent-consult deferral ('I want to have a little discussion with my parents')
time-to-think requests ('I need to have at least 24 hours of time to think about it')
scholarship asks ('would there be any scope of any like a scholarship')
placement scepticism ('placement assistance but not 100% placement')
EMI affordability talk ('you can pay it in EMI also I'm comfortable with that')
scarcity/deadline counters from the counsellor ('we are left with the 70 seats', 'Today is the last day of the counseling')
live parent calls pushed mid-session ('Mute yourself, okay? And call your parents, tell about the fee structure')

phases.name: Close
markers: the 4,000 seat-blocking / booking amount ('pay some amount of money... which is about 4,000')
refundability check ('Is that 4,000 refundable?')
dashboard/UPI payment walkthrough in-session ('activate directly on your dashboard'; 'I'm not going to share you any payment links because a lot of scams were happening')
same-day deadline urgency ('Today is the last day of the registration')
EMI/finance team handoff ('That is EMI team who will be calling you')
WhatsApp confirmations, brochure and welcome message sent
LMS onboarding and congratulations after payment
follow-up call or callback scheduling when no commitment is made

openingPatterns: Audibility check first ('can you hear me?' / repeated 'Hello'), then camera-on request — the dominant opener across the corpus
Camera/mic check, then counsellor introduces himself as 'Senior Academic Counsellor' for the BITS/IIM Business Analytics with Gen AI programme and asks for an introduction and motivation
Counsellor self-introduction with social proof ('6,000+ students counselled') before collecting student background and goals
Formal greeting plus agenda-setting: counsellor lists all topics to be covered — program, curriculum, fee structure, faculty, timing
Greet student by name, confirm email ID and that they applied for Business Analytics & AI, congratulate on shortlisting, then ask why they applied and whether parents know about the course
Extended audio/camera troubleshooting consuming the first stretch of the call (network issues, reconnects, offers to call back) before any programme discussion

paymentAskNorms.notes: The ask lands about three-quarters into the call (median 78% of call length; interquartile 72-84%), framed as a 4,000 (occasionally 2,000) booking amount to 'secure/block your seat' with the balance moved to EMI handled by a separate finance team. Converting calls almost always contain an explicit ask (87% vs 65% overall) and push it to completion in the same session: the counsellor walks the student through their own dashboard/UPI ('I'm not going to share you any payment links because a lot of scams were happening'), manufactures same-call urgency ('Today is the last day of the registration', 'we are left with the 70 seats'), and often has the student mute and call a parent mid-session for approval before paying live on the call.
